channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: White House chief of staff meets with Anthropic CEO over its new AI technology
item.description: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles has met with Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to discuss the company's new AI model, Mythos.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: IPv6 may briefly have accounted for more than half of internet traffic
item.description: Has IPv6 finally reached its day of glory? It’s fair to say that IPv6 has not had the level of take-up expected when the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) ratified it back in 1998. Take-up has been agonizingly slow, not reaching 5 percent of traffic until 2014.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Robot Zuckerberg shows how IT can free up CEOs’ time
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg, the CEO of Meta, is building an AI version of himself. The virtual CEO is being trained on Zuckerberg’s mannerisms and will be loaded with his views on corporate strategy, the Financial Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How scammers target grieving victims through online games
item.description: Games like Words With Friends have become hunting grounds for romance scammers who build trust and then ask victims for untraceable gift card payments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The stigma around AI in journalism may be easing, but trust is still fragile
item.description: I tend to write about AI from the perspective of the bleeding edge, looking at how journalists and media companies are using the technology to change the way they work, reach new audiences, and transform their organizations.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: How to think about Apple Business
item.description: Apple Business is aimed at small businesses coalesced around Macs, iPhones, and iPads. If that’s you, and all your systems are made by Apple, the service is likely to be all you need to run a small operation of up to a few dozen seats.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI isn’t built for all languages and cultures. There’s a push to fix that
item.description: Egyptian coder Assem Sabry has long wanted an AI model that represents his culture. The problem is he hasn’t been able to find one. “The AI industry in Egypt . . . doesn’t exist,” Sabry says. So he built his own: Horus, named after the ancient Egyptian god of the sky.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: The padel app turning matches into meet-cutes
item.description: Padel has taken the sports world by storm. In a smaller but growing circle, it’s also become a way to date. Much of that runs through Playtomic, a booking app for racquet sports where players join “open matches” with strangers, chat through the app, and meet people they wouldn’t otherwise encounter.
item.dc:creator: Hauwa Otori, Fast Company
